title: [lichen_l2/lora_l2] callback registered before link_ctx fully initialized
description:
Cross-file race condition: In lichen_l2_iface_init(), the RX callback is registered (line 978) BEFORE link_ctx_initialized is set to 1 (line 991).

Race scenario:
1. lichen_l2_iface_init() calls lichen_link_init() at line 959
2. Callback registered at line 978
3. LoRa packet arrives
4. RX thread invokes lora_rx_callback()
5. lora_rx_callback checks lichen_iface (set at line 975) - OK
6. lichen_l2_input checks link_ctx_initialized - still 0!
7. Frame dropped with 'RX before link_ctx initialized' warning

The window is small (between lines 978 and 991), but during this window:
- Callback is registered
- lichen_iface is set
- link_ctx IS initialized (line 959)
- But link_ctx_initialized flag is NOT yet set

The comment at line 987-991 says 'Set AFTER RX callback registration so the flag truthfully indicates that the full initialization sequence is complete' - but this creates a window where valid frames are dropped.

Location: lichen_l2.c:978-991

Recommendation: Set link_ctx_initialized=1 BEFORE registering the callback. The 'truthful indication' is less important than not dropping valid frames.
close_reason: Fixed: state transitions are now atomic and validated
